trade.summary: Germania and Svezia are major trading partners. Germania exports vehicles, machinery, chemicals, and electrical equipment; Svezia exports vehicles, paper products, iron and steel, machinery, and telecommunications equipment. Both are deeply integrated in EU single market supply chains.

diplomatic.summary: Germania and Svezia have excellent diplomatic relations. Svezia joined NATO in 2024, deepening the bilateral security relationship. Both are EU members and coordinate closely on EU policy. Shared values around democracy, multilateralism, and sustainability. Strong cooperation on Ucraina support and sanctions enforcement.

military.summary: Svezia joined NATO in March 2024, significantly deepening Germania-Svezia military cooperation. Joint Baltic Sea security operations, cooperation on submarine detection, and NATO planning are now fully integrated. Both contribute to NATO Eastern Flank reinforcement. Svezia's Gripen fighter cooperates with German Eurofighter in NATO air policing.

financial.summary: Strong bilateral investment. Swedish firms (Volvo, Ericsson, IKEA, H&M, Spotify) have significant German operations. German firms invest heavily in Svezia, particularly in automotive (Volkswagen-Scania ownership) and manufacturing. Svezia's krona-euro relationship creates some financial linkage without full eurozone membership.

energy.summary: Both are major renewable energy investors. Svezia exports electricity (nuclear and hydro) via Baltic interconnectors that benefit Germania's grid. Both are developing offshore wind in the Baltic Sea. Germania views Svezia as a key partner in the European energy transition and grid interconnection.

scientific.summary: Exceptionally strong scientific collaboration. German and Swedish research institutions and universities partner extensively across all disciplines. Shared Horizon Europe participation. Svezia and Germania are both among Europe's top scientific producers. Joint industrial research in automotive, materials, and life sciences.
